mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals a cooling labor market that reduces wage inflation pressures, prompting expectations of central bank policy easing and triggering positive equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative or flat in >70% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent for three consecutive observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real savings and declining inflation indices expand household real purchasing power, directly boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window when RSAFS rises and CPIAUCSL declines concurrently, or 60-day returns are negative in >65% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in industrial production indicate immediate supply chain constraints or demand destruction, triggering short-term risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns follow INDPRO declines exceeding 0.5 percent in >60% of instances during documented monetary tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month inflation readings exceeding 0.3 percent confirm persistent price pressures, forcing markets to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy and generating negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.3 percent, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and expanding consumer demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150 thousand, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.04 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and rising federal funds rates signal aggressive credit tightening in the real estate sector, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where HOUST declines and FEDFUNDS rises simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annually during late-cycle expansions indicates demand outpacing productive capacity, generating inflationary pressures and eventual policy tightening that negatively impacts equities.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns follow GDPC1 annual growth rates above 2.5 percent in >65% of observations during late-cycle phases, or regime-specific IC is negative.
